primary_diagnosis.name: Severe interdigital tinea pedis with maceration and fissuring (moccasin pattern)

pathophysiology_summary: Tinea pedis is a dermatophyte infection of the feet; chronic interdigital disease with heat, sweat and occlusive footwear causes white maceration and painful deep fissuring of the toe-webs, plus a diffuse fine 'moccasin' scaling/hyperkeratosis of the soles and lateral feet. The deep macerated fissure is a portal of entry for secondary bacterial cellulitis/lymphangitis. Discriminators: the moccasin scaling and web-space localisation distinguish it from a shoe contact dermatitis (which favours the dorsum), and the moccasin distribution distinguishes dermatophyte tinea from candidal intertrigo (confined to moist webs). Management is a topical antifungal (terbinafine) to the whole foot for weeks, a brief combination antifungal/mild-steroid to settle the acutely inflamed fissure, meticulous drying (between toes), moisture-wicking socks changed mid-shift, footwear/antifungal-powder measures, and treating any concurrent tinea cruris. The must-not-miss is secondary cellulitis (ascending hot erythema, red streaks, fever), which is screened negative here — so the priority is a strong cellulitis safety-net given the open fissure.

differential.evidence_basis: Chronic web-space maceration/fissuring with moccasin scaling.
The fissure is a portal — screen for ascending hot erythema/red streaks/fever.
Would favour the dorsum, not the web spaces/soles.
Confined to moist webs without moccasin scaling.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Diagnose tinea pedis with fissuring, screen for ascending cellulitis, and treat antifungally with strict drying; strong cellulitis safety-net given the open fissure.

differential.evidence_basis: Moccasin scaling + macerated fissure; no ascending cellulitis.
No ascending erythema/red streaks/fever — remains the key safety-net.
Working diagnosis: whole-foot antifungal + brief combination for the fissure + strict drying/footwear; cellulitis safety-net.

red_flags.description: Secondary bacterial cellulitis/lymphangitis through the fissure (ascending hot erythema, red streaks up the leg, fever)
red_flags.clinical_significance: The deep fissure is a dangerous portal for streptococcal/staphylococcal cellulitis.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Not screening/safety-netting for ascending cellulitis through the open fissure.
Antifungal without strict drying/footwear change, so it recurs.
Using the combination steroid beyond a few days (masking tinea).

telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Whole-foot antifungal + brief combination for the fissure + strict drying/footwear; urgent review/ED if ascending cellulitis (hot spreading erythema, red streaks, fever).
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Missing an evolving cellulitis, or failing to keep the feet dry.

guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Tinea pedis: topical antifungal for weeks (whole foot for moccasin type); keep feet dry; treat footwear/socks; oral antifungal for extensive/refractory disease; watch for secondary bacterial infection.
